name: hsmodels
documentation: Refer to the models section of https://hydroshare.github.io/hsclient/

functions.name: hsmodels.schemas.validators.validate_user_id
documentation: Validate that value is a valid id for a hydroshare user.

methods.name: hsmodels.schemas.base_models.BaseMetadata.model_dump
documentation:
Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.

Checks the config for a schema_config dictionary_field and converts a dictionary to a list of key/value pairs.
This converts the dictionary to a format that can be described in a json schema (which can be found below in the
schema_extra staticmethod.

Override the default of exclude_none to True

methods.name: hsmodels.schemas.base_models.BaseMetadata.model_dump_json
documentation:
Generate a JSON representation of the model, `include` and `exclude` arguments as per `dict()`.

`encoder` is an optional function to supply as `default` to json.dumps(), other arguments as per `json.dumps()`.

Override the default of exclude_none to True

modules.name: hsmodels.schemas.languages_iso
documentation: The complete language code info is from http://www.loc.gov/standards/iso639-2/php/code_list.php
Language which has both "B" and "T" code will just use the "B" code in the list below
code "nob", "pro","vol" have corresponding languages which include non-English character

documentation: 8-bit byte that contains uninterpreted character data

documentation: signed integer (32bit)

class_variables.name: hsmodels.schemas.enums.VariableType.Float
documentation: floating point (32bit)

class_variables.name: hsmodels.schemas.enums.VariableType.Double
documentation: floating point(64bit)

class_variables.name: hsmodels.schemas.enums.VariableType.Int64

class_variables.name: hsmodels.schemas.enums.VariableType.String
documentation: variable length character string

class_variables.name: hsmodels.schemas.enums.VariableType.User_Defined_Type

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.fields.UTCOffSet

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.fields.BoxCoverage
documentation:
A class used to represent geographic coverage metadata for a resource or aggregation expressed as a

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.fields.PointSpatialReference

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.fields.PeriodCoverage

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.fields.ModelProgramFile

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.resource.ResourceMetadataIn
documentation: A class used to represent the metadata for a resource

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.GeographicRasterMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a geographic raster aggregation

A geographic raster aggregation consists of the multiple content files that make up a
geographic raster dataset to which aggregation-level metadata have been added. Rasters
may have multiple files and multiple bands and are stored in HydroShare as GeoTIFF files.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.GeographicFeatureMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a geographic feature aggregation

A geographic feature aggregation consists of the multiple content files that make up an
ESRI shapefile containing a geographic feature dataset and to which aggregation-level
metadata have been added.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.MultidimensionalMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a multidimensional space-time aggregation

A multidimensional aggregation consists of a Network Common Data Form (NetCDF) file that
makes up a multidimensional space-time dataset to which aggregation-level metadata have
been added.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.ReferencedTimeSeriesMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a referenced time series aggregation

A referenced time series aggregation consists of references to specific time series
datasets hosted on an external web service to which aggregation-level metadata have
been added.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.FileSetMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a file set aggregation

A file set aggregation consists of an arbitrary collection of files that are logically
grouped together as an aggregation and to which aggregation-level metadata have been
added. There may be any number of files in the aggregation, and files may be of any type.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.SingleFileMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a single file aggregation

A single file aggregation consists of a single content file to which aggregation-level
metadata have been added.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.TimeSeriesMetadataIn
documentation:
A class used to represent the metadata associated with a time series aggregation

A time series aggregation consists of one or more time series datasets to which
aggregation-level metadata have been added. Time series datasets in HydroShare
consist of sequences of individual data values that are ordered in time to record
the changing trend of a certain phenomenon. They are stored in HydroShare using
ODM2 SQLite database files.

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.ModelProgramMetadataIn
documentation: A class used to represent the metadata associated with a model program aggregation

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.ModelInstanceMetadataIn
documentation: A class used to represent the metadata associated with a model instance aggregation

classes.name: hsmodels.schemas.aggregations.CSVFileMetadataIn
documentation: A class used to represent the metadata associated with a CSV aggregation
